displayTitle: Silver Seeds and the Sharpness Floor

beats.narration: Every radiograph is born inside the emulsion — billions of silver bromide grains. Let's watch radiation write an invisible image.

payload.title: THE LATENT IMAGE
payload.sub: How radiation writes an invisible image in silver.

beats.narration: Radiation frees electrons. A sensitivity speck traps one, attracts a silver ion — one silver atom. Repeat: a few atoms make the grain developable.

payload.heading: Trap an electron, draw a silver ion
payload.note: A FEW SILVER ATOMS MAKE THE GRAIN DEVELOPABLE

beats.narration: Development converts each seeded grain entirely to black silver. Fixer dissolves the unexposed grains. A few atoms gating a billion — film's built-in amplifier.
But electrons, not photons, expose film. They travel before stopping, so every edge smears — inherent unsharpness, and it grows with radiation energy.

payload.heading: Electrons, not photons, expose film
payload.note: HIGHER ENERGY → LONGER RANGE → MORE SMEAR

beats.narration: Energy sets a sharpness floor geometry can't beat. Once geometry's below it, stretching distance buys almost nothing. Know the floor — spend effort where it counts.

payload.sub: Spend your sharpness effort where it counts.
